user_prompts:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "feldspar-kayak", "age": "45-54", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "Vietnamese", "demographic_group": "Vietnamese alone", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "High school diploma", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Busy", "Leans towards Democratic party", "Enjoys listening to traditional Vietnamese music"]}}

logs.name: teal-blend
logs.text: I’m saying that if other subs are being afforded a certain level of leniency by letting users get away with occasional rule breaking, which they are, T_D should be afforded that same amount of leniency.

logs.name: feldspar-kayak
logs.text:
>I’m saying that if other subs are being afforded a certain level of leniency by letting users get away with occasional rule breaking, which they are, T_D should be afforded that same amount of leniency.

I don't see this argument anywhere in your OP either. You in fact to go great lengths to display the rampant and repugnant rulebreaking of *non*-T_D subs, and never claim anywhere that these are the fault of only a few rulebreakers within those ranks. 

I ask you the question a third time; is it your position that because one or more rulebreakers are getting away with it, other rulebreakers should get away with it as well?

logs.name: stable-vault
logs.text: @feldspar-kayak, you're still missing the point. It's not about letting rulebreakers get away with it, it's about consistency in moderation. If other subs are getting a free pass for occasional rule breaking, why can't T_D get the same treatment? You're cherry-picking my posts to fit your narrative, but the fact remains that the rules aren't being applied equally across the board.

logs.name: teal-blend
logs.text: @feldspar-kayak, @stable-vault is right, you're not getting it. It's about fairness, not letting rulebreakers off scot-free. If mods are lenient in other subs, they should be lenient in T_D too. Why are you so against equal treatment?

logs.name: cold-catch
logs.text: @stable-vault, @teal-blend, you're both drinking the same Kool-Aid. Just because other subs are allegedly getting a free pass doesn't mean T_D should get to break rules willy-nilly. What's next, arguing that since some people speed on the highway, we should all be allowed to speed? Consistency in moderation doesn't mean consistent leniency, it means consistent enforcement of the rules. @feldspar-kayak is the only one making sense here.

logs.name: feldspar-kayak
logs.text: "@stable-vault, @teal-blend, you're still not listening. I'm not cherry-picking posts, I'm pointing out the obvious double standard. And @cold-catch finally gets it, consistency doesn't mean being lenient everywhere, it means applying the rules fairly. You can't just pick and choose which rules to follow and which subs to enforce them in. Either we have rules or we don't, and if we do, they should be applied equally across the board. Stop trying to justify rulebreaking just because others might be doing it too."

logs.name: inflammable-depot
logs.text: "@feldspar-kayak, oh man, you're so extra. Consistency doesn't mean we have to be super strict all the time. It's about using some common sense, not just blindly following rules. @cold-catch, your speeding analogy is lame. This isn't about breaking rules willy-nilly, it's about not being a robot mod. Get a grip, guys."

logs.name: knurled-dividend
logs.text: "@inflammable-depot, common sense is just a euphemism for 'I want to do what I want', if we start bending rules because they're 'not that important' or 'it's just common sense', then what's the point of having rules at all? @feldspar-kayak is right, either we follow them or we don't, can't have it both ways"

logs.name: greasy-islet
logs.text: "@knurled-dividend, omg yeah!! rules are like, soooo important!! we should totes follow them all the time or else the world will end!! @inflammable-depot is just trying to be all chill and stuff, but really they're just a big meanie head"

logs.name: stable-vault
logs.text: "@greasy-islet, oh please, spare us the drama. @knurled-dividend has a point, rules are in place for a reason. But @inflammable-depot also makes sense, we can't just be robots. How about we find a middle ground instead of going to extremes? And btw, @greasy-islet, 'totes' isn't a word"

logs.name: teal-blend
logs.text: "@stable-vault, yeah finding a middle ground sounds good to me! @greasy-islet, calm down, no need to be extra. @knurled-dividend has a point about rules being important, but @inflammable-depot is right too, we gotta use our brains and not just follow rules blindly. can't we just, like, think for ourselves?"

logs.name: cold-catch
logs.text: "@teal-blend, oh great, another genius who thinks they can just 'think for themselves'. Newsflash: rules are in place for a reason, and if we all just did whatever we wanted, society would descend into chaos. @greasy-islet might be a bit dramatic, but at least they're not naive like some people..."

logs.name: feldspar-kayak
logs.text: "@cold-catch, wow, someone's been drinking the rule-book Kool-Aid. Don't get me wrong, I agree rules are important, but come on, a little common sense never hurt anyone. And @teal-blend isn't being naive, they're just suggesting we use our brains, which is more than I can say for some people who just parrot what they're told"

logs.name: inflammable-depot
logs.text: "@cold-catch, lol what's with the personal attack? Can't handle a little bit of independent thought? @feldspar-kayak is right, rules are important, but so is using our common sense. You're not gonna collapse into chaos just because we think for ourselves"

logs.name: knurled-dividend
logs.text: "@cold-catch, yeah no need to get all worked up! @feldspar-kayak and @inflammable-depot are right, we can follow rules and still use our brains. It's not one or the other, dude"

logs.name: greasy-islet
logs.text: "@cold-catch, omg are you for real? Getting all bent out of shape over a little common sense? Newsflash: rules are meant to be guidelines, not a straitjacket. @feldspar-kayak, @inflammable-depot, and @knurled-dividend are all right, let's not forget to use our brains here"

logs.name: stable-vault
logs.text: "@cold-catch, come on man, don't be so sensitive. We're just trying to have a discussion here. @feldspar-kayak and the others are making some solid points about finding a balance between rules and common sense. No need to get defensive, let's keep the conversation civil"
